title: Who Is This Agent? Machine Identity Security
modules: Defining Machine Identity: How AI Agents Authenticate and Act on Your Behalf
Credential and Token Management for Autonomous AI Workflows
Privilege Escalation and Lateral Movement via Compromised Agent Identities
Implementing Zero-Trust Principles for Non-Human Principals
Auditing and Monitoring Agent Behavior at Scale
synopsis: AI agents are being granted real credentials, API keys, and system privileges — yet most identity governance frameworks were built for humans, leaving machine identities dangerously under-managed. This course teaches security and IAM professionals exactly how agent identities are exploited and how to close those gaps with modern controls.
rationale: Non-human identities now outnumber human identities in many enterprises, yet purpose-built governance for AI agents remains an almost entirely unaddressed blind spot in most IAM programs.
learning_outcome: Learners can audit an AI agent deployment for identity risks, apply zero-trust principles to non-human principals, and configure monitoring to detect anomalous agent credential use.
source_signals: Keeper Security: The identity security platform for humans, machines, and AI agents
Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification
Zero-Click Attacks: AI Agents and the Next Cybersecurity Challenge
